name: New England Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 lb Firm tart apples (Granny Smith, Pippin, Golden Delicious, Rhode Island, Northern Spy, as available)
1/3 c Granulated sugar (light brown sugar or combination)
1 ts Finely grated lemon zest (yellow peel only)
recipeInstructions:
Flaky Pie Dough: Combine flour, salt, and baking powder in a work bowl of food processor. Pulse 2 to 3 times to mix. Cut butter into 8 pieces and add to the bowl. Pulse rapidly about 15 times until the mixture resembles coarse cornmeal and no large pieces of butter remain visible. Add 2 tablespoons water and pulse 4 or 5 times to mix, checking that most but not all of the dough forms a ball. If the dough remains dry, add remaining 1 tablespoon water, sprinkling it on dough and pulsing again 4 or 5 times. Remove dough from processor and quickly form into a ball with floured hands. Place dough on a piece of lightly floured plastic wrap. Lightly flour the top of the dough and cover with plastic wrap. Press with the palm of the hand to form the dough into a 6-inch diameter disk. Refrigerate the dough while preparing the topping.
Crumb Topping: Combine brown sugar and flour in a mixing bowl. Add butter and stir evenly. Set the mixture aside for 5 minutes, then using fingertips, break it into 1/4- to 1/2-inch crumbs. Set aside.
Dough Preparation: Remove dough from the refrigerator and place on a flat work surface. Roll the dough, still between sheets of plastic, to a 12-inch disk. Peel off the top plastic and ease the dough into a 9-inch pie pan. Gently press the dough into the pan and peel off the top plastic. Trim excess dough within 1/2-inch of the pan edge, turning it under around the edges; flute the edges. Chill the pie shell while preparing the filling.
Filling: Peel, halve, and core apples; cut into 1/2-inch pieces. Place apples in a bowl with sugar, flour, cinnamon, and lemon zest, tossing well to combine. Place the filling in the dough-lined pan, mounding it slightly in the center. Dot the filling with butter and cover with the reserved Crumb Topping. Bake the pie on the bottom rack at 400 degrees for 15 minutes. Lower the temperature to 350 degrees and move the pie to the middle rack. Bake for 20 to 30 minutes longer, until the crust edges are deep golden brown and juices just begin to bubble up. Cool the pie on a rack and serve warm or at room temperature. Makes 8 servings.
